county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Washington County formed 1855 as an original Kansas Territory county. County seat is Washington. Named for George Washington. Northern Kansas bordering Nebraska. Hollenberg Pony Express Station (only original unaltered station). Oregon/California Trail corridor. Czech, Scandinavian, and German settlers.
county_metadata.research_tips: Washington County Register of Deeds in Washington holds land records from 1855. The Washington County Historical Society and Museum has local genealogical collections. BLM GLO land patents. Scandinavian, German, and Czech settlers left church records from the 1860s-1880s. Probate Court records from 1855. Washington newspapers at KSHS. FamilySearch has microfilmed some Washington County records. The county borders Nebraska; cross-border family research may be needed. Hollenberg Pony Express Station (the only original unaltered station) is in the county -- historical records at KSHS. District Court naturalization records document immigrant arrivals.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Washington County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Washington

source_profiles.source_name: Washington County Deed Books -- Register of Deeds
source_profiles.repository: Washington County Register of Deeds, Washington
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original deed books for Washington County from 1855; grantor/grantee indexes; mortgages; liens; Register of Deeds office maintains land records
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival from 1855; deed books are substantially complete

source_profiles.repository: Washington County Probate Court, Washington
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, inventories, guardianships, and estate settlements for Washington County; Probate Court has jurisdiction over estate matters

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Declarations of intention and petitions for naturalization filed in Washington County District Court; document immigrant arrivals and origins; held at the courthouse or transferred to KSHS

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Wichita County formed 1873, organized 1886. County seat is Leoti. Named for the Wichita people. Not to be confused with the city of Wichita (Sedgwick County). County seat war between Leoti and Coronado in the 1880s. Western Kansas. Dust Bowl effects. Irrigation agriculture.
county_metadata.research_tips: Wichita County Register of Deeds in Leoti holds land records from the 1880s. Not to be confused with the city of Wichita (in Sedgwick County). The county experienced a violent county seat war between Leoti and Coronado in the 1880s. The Wichita County Historical Society and Museum in Leoti has local collections. BLM GLO land patents are essential for tracing homesteaders. Probate Court records from the 1880s. Church records from pioneer congregations. Leoti Standard newspaper at KSHS. FamilySearch has limited microfilmed records. Dust Bowl effects were severe. Irrigation agriculture brought later families.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Wichita County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Leoti

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original deed books for Wichita County from 1873; grantor/grantee indexes; mortgages; liens; Register of Deeds office maintains land records
source_profiles.survival_notes: Records begin from practical settlement in the 1873s-1883s

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Wilson County formed 1855 as an original Kansas Territory county. County seat is Fredonia. Named for Hiero T. Wilson, territorial legislator. Neodesha had the first commercial oil well west of the Mississippi (1892). Verdigris River valley. Southeastern Kansas.
county_metadata.research_tips: Wilson County Register of Deeds in Fredonia holds land records from 1855. The Wilson County Historical Society and Museum in Fredonia has local genealogical collections. BLM GLO land patents. The county had early oil production (1860s) -- one of the first oil-producing areas in Kansas. Church records from Methodist, Baptist, and Christian Church congregations from the 1850s. Probate Court records from 1855. Fredonia newspapers at KSHS. FamilySearch has microfilmed Wilson County records. The Verdigris River valley was the settlement corridor. Neodesha was the site of the first commercial oil well west of the Mississippi (1892).
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Wilson County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Fredonia

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Woodson County formed 1855 as an original Kansas Territory county. County seat is Yates Center. Named for Daniel Woodson, acting territorial governor. Flint Hills region. Toronto Lake construction displaced some communities. Oil production. Small population.
county_metadata.research_tips: Woodson County Register of Deeds in Yates Center holds land records from 1855. The Woodson County Historical Society has local genealogical collections. BLM GLO land patents. Church records from Methodist, Baptist, and Presbyterian congregations from the 1850s-1860s. Probate Court records from 1855. Yates Center newspapers at KSHS. FamilySearch has microfilmed some Woodson County records. The county is in the Flint Hills with ranching records at KSHS. Toronto Lake construction displaced some communities; check KSHS for relocation records. Oil production from the early 1900s.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Woodson County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Yates Center

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Wyandotte County formed 1859. County seat is Kansas City (unified government with Kansas City, KS since 1997). Named for the Wyandot people who had a reservation here. Major meatpacking and railroad center. Diverse immigrant communities (Croatian, Serbian, Mexican, African American). Quindaro was a Free State port and Underground Railroad site. Argentine and Rosedale were independent cities before consolidation.
county_metadata.research_tips: Wyandotte County Register of Deeds in Kansas City holds land records from 1859. Kansas City, Kansas was a major meatpacking and railroad center -- Armour, Swift, and other packing plant records may document workers. The Wyandotte County Historical Society and Museum has local collections. The county is named for the Wyandot people who had a reservation here before Kansas Territory -- Wyandot cemetery and tribal records at NARA. BLM GLO land patents. Diverse immigrant communities (Croatian, Serbian, Mexican, African American) left varied church and fraternal organization records. Probate Court records from 1859. Kansas City Kansan newspaper at KSHS. FamilySearch has microfilmed Wyandotte County records. Argentine and Rosedale were independent cities before consolidation. Quindaro was a Free State port and Underground Railroad site -- records at KSHS and KU.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Wyandotte County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Kansas City
